title: Pillars of nuclear non-proliferation treaty are being undermined
subhed: The 11th Review Conference of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons, held every five years, is set to begin Monday in New York in a tense atmosphere. In addition to the war waged against Iran by the US and Israel, all pillars of the treaty have become fragile.

France to reimburse cadmium tests, but doctors warn access is 'too restrictive'
subhed: This carcinogenic heavy metal affects most people living in France, but the screening, expected to be reimbursed by the government starting this summer, will be limited to those considered 'potentially overexposed' because of where they live.

title: Man who planned shooting at Washington press gala targeted Trump administration officials, authorities say
subhed: The suspect, Cole Tomas Allen, who is set to appear in court as early as Monday, wrote a manifesto in which he expressed his anger toward the government and mocked the event's security measures.

title: Charles III visits the US in bid to repair the 'special relationship' between London and Washington
subhed: The British royal couple is expected in the US capital on Monday for a four-day visit, as tensions have mounted between the two countries.

title: Oil traders' windfall profits highlight paradoxes of Swiss model
subhed: Geneva is full of rumors about the record profits made by oil market players. The legal framework in which they operate has drawn criticism and sparked calls for tighter regulation.

subhed: 'I wouldn't be where I am today if...' In this series, Le Monde interviews a public figure about a decisive moment in their life.  The internationally renowned visual artist recalls the strange atmosphere of the seaside town of Berck, where she grew up.

title: Should fiction be faithful to history? Film director Xavier Giannoli and historian Tal Bruttmann discuss
subhed: Xavier Giannoli's latest film, 'Les Rayons et les Ombres,' is a critical and box office success. It has also sparked questions about its approach to the character of Jean Luchaire, a collaborator during World War II. Can a work of art take liberties with the facts, especially when dealing with such a sensitive period?
